items.title: Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police

children.content: A man is in a serious but stable condition after being shot by police during an altercation in Hobart's northern suburbs early on Sunday morning.

gallery.altText: Two police cars parked on a street with a drone in the air

thumbnail.altText: Two police cars parked on a street with a drone in the air

items.title: Fuel crisis driving some farmers to consider abandoning harvests

children.content: Tasmanian farmers are considering not harvesting crops like broccoli and cauliflower because increasing freight costs are proving to be so debilitating.

image.altText: a tractor is parked next to a diesel fuel pump

items.title: From Hobart to the world stage: Theatre community mourns death of a giant

children.content: A giant of Australian theatre who helped launch the careers of some of the country's biggest names of the stage has died aged 93.

image.altText: A man in a suit looks at the camera.

items.title: 'Chock-a-block' cemetery means non-Catholics have no grave options

children.content: The Anglican church in a small northern Tasmanian town has exhausted all its cemetery space after selling off land to accommodate new social and affordable housing.

gallery.altText: A woman in a blue cardigan stands in a cemetery on a cloudy day.
A woman in a white t-shirt and blue jeans sits alongside a woman wearing black by a grave adorned with pink flowers.
Drone image of Campbell Town, Tasmania

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a blue cardigan stands in a cemetery on a cloudy day.
A woman in a white t-shirt and blue jeans sits alongside a woman wearing black by a grave adorned with pink flowers.

items.title: Activist ship deliberately collides with trawler as 'krill wars' escalate

children.content: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

image.altText: View from the deck of a ship as it crashes into another ship.

items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering purchasing an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

image.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

items.title: Stagflation is already happening, a leading economist warns

children.content: With stagflation risks rising, what lessons could the treasurer learn from the past?

image.altText: An empty sign on petrol pumps

items.title: Motorists urged to dob in service stations over fuel price hikes

children.content: The NSW government fast-tracks a compliance blitz to strengthen fuel price transparency and protect motorists from misleading pricing.

image.altText: a NSW Fair Trading officer holding a mobile phone carrying out an inspection

items.title: Arnhem Land rock art raises questions about mainland Tasmanian tigers

children.content: Newly documented rock art in Arnhem Land has raised questions about how long Tasmanian tigers lived on the mainland before they were wiped out.

items.title: Police look for missing cocaine after ocean 'drop-off' during 'bizarre' journey

children.content: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

image.altText: A helicoper flies over a ship carrying drugs

items.title: Walsh facing up to six weeks on sidelines with fractured cheekbone

children.content: The Brisbane Broncos are dealt a massive blow in their NRL premiership defence, with Reece Walsh set for a lengthy stint away from the game.

image.altText: A rugby league player in white holds his face and walks off

items.title: Whole villages in Sumatra erased overnight — and still living in the wreckage

children.content: Months after floods killed more than a thousand people across Sumatra, residents are still digging their way out of the wreckage. While survivors are eager to rebuild, many just can’t shake the fear that the next catastrophe is just around the corner.

gallery.altText: A lone house stands in the middle of debris and logs in Sawang district.
Drone footage showing seas of timber throughout Geudumbak — an area east of Sawang district in North Aceh.
A sea of timber running throughout North Aceh after the floods.

thumbnail.altText: A lone house stands in the middle of debris and logs in Sawang district.
Drone footage showing seas of timber throughout Geudumbak — an area east of Sawang district in North Aceh.
A sea of timber running throughout North Aceh after the floods.

items.title: Queen at 100 is honoured in fashion exhibition as Andrew scandal bites

children.content: Within palace walls the Queen was a godlike figure. As her glorious fashion goes on display to commemorate what would have been her 100th birthday, how would she have responded to the Andrew crisis if alive today?

image.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

items.title: After NT spent 'ridiculous' millions on aircraft, operations move offshore

children.content: A modern version of a 1960s-era aircraft that can land on sea and snow was meant to be built in NSW, and then in the NT. But years later it still hasn’t taken off.

image.altText: A blue plane over the map of the Australia, and images  of Darwin City

items.title: Artemis crew enter day four with plans to take manual control of spacecraft

children.content: The Artemis astronauts have been taking a moment to look back at Earth as they continue deep into space toward the Moon.

gallery.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.

thumbnail.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.

items.title: The 'mudcrabs' battling to remediate Sydney's sickest waterway

children.content: Scurrying about the banks of Sydney's Cooks River, an army of volunteers known as the 'mudcrabs' is working to bring one of the nation's most toxic waterways back to life.

image.altText: A number of men, women and young people working together to care for vegetation on the side of a path.

items.title: I don't have all day to watch you struggle at an internet quiz

children.content: It's painfully obvious you're going to fail this quiz, so let's get it over with and return to our Sunday mornings, shall we?

image.altText: Tom Gleeson standing with his arms crossed with a serious look on his face with th Hard quiz logo in the top left corner

items.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for 'Easter eggs' yields stunning images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

gallery.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.

thumbnail.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
A close-up of a man holding a camera.

items.title: Thom Yorke's solo tour was one of a kind. This Aussie film captured it

children.content: The award-winning Australian team behind a new concert film of the Radiohead frontman's debut solo tour discusses how it happened and why it matters.

image.altText: Thom Yorke reaches for a knob on a raised piece of equipment, performing live on stage, bathed in blue light

items.title: Too young for social media, but old enough to be locked up

children.content: As a nation, we have decided that a teenager cannot navigate a social media algorithm, but a 10-year-old can navigate a courtroom. For Australia's 2025 UN youth representative Satara Uthayakumaran, the contradiction is impossible to ignore.

image.altText: A young women sits listening to young unidentified detainees in a youth detention centre

items.title: Albany woman smashes WA's record for heaviest pumpkin with 408kg feat

children.content: A pumpkin grown in Albany has beaten the previous WA record for heaviest pumpkin by nearly 60 kilograms.

gallery.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck

thumbnail.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck

items.title: 'Doesn't make any sense': Young Aussies can't justify fuel costs to get to work

children.content: Young people are taking extraordinary steps to deal with the fuel crisis, including cutting back on work because the cost of fuel to get there is now higher than the money they will earn for the shift.

image.altText: A petrol station

items.title: Australian helps Oxford end women's Boat Race drought

children.content: Competing for Oxford in the historic women's Boat Race for a fourth time, Australia's Annie Anezakis finally tastes victory.

image.altText: Annie Anezakis celebrates Oxford Iniveristy winning women's Boat Race.

items.title: Regurgitated citrus and a 'nearly dislocated' shoulder: Lisa Millar takes on Gayndah

children.content: The big cities may have their Easter shows and Ekkas, but Lisa Millar was reminded why nothing beats the small-town festival while filming the latest episode of Back Roads in Gayndah.

gallery.altText: Lisa Millar smiles while leaning over a fence on a farm and talking to someone seen from the side.

thumbnail.altText: Lisa Millar smiles while leaning over a fence on a farm and talking to someone seen from the side.

avatar.alt: A woman with brown hair wearing a yellow patterned shirt smiles at the camera.

items.title: Three dead, two critical in spate of crashes on Queensland roads

children.content: The state's road toll for 2026 is at least 80, compared to 59 by April 1 last year.

image.altText: A night time photo of a Queensland Police Service car with its blue light illuminated.

items.title: Brazilian admits to 'fan-girling' during win over Gilmore at Bells Beach

children.content: Luana Silva is overwhelmed to defeat one of her idols, Australia's eight-time world champion Stephanie Gilmore, in the first round.

image.altText: Stephanie Gilmore suring at the Bells Beach in 2026.

items.title: Cooking group battling loneliness through shared 'love language'

children.content: At a community house just outside of Brisbane, people living alone come together and turn donated food into gourmet meals.

gallery.altText: Group of mature-aged people eating at a table

thumbnail.altText: Group of mature-aged people eating at a table

items.title: 'Amazing' breakthrough as rare bird relearns its lost 'love song'

children.content: There are only about 250 regent honeyeaters left in the wild, and they have been losing their unique call, mimicking other birds instead. Conservationists say they are now successfully teaching young birds their traditional song.

image.altText: Regent honeyeater and wattle bird

items.title: Sunk twice and refloated in flood, restoration begins on paddleboat

children.content: A century-old wooden paddleboat sitting on the banks of an outback river has quite the story to tell, and a restoration will ensure its journey continues.

image.altText: Two-storey timber paddle boat sits on the banks of the Thompson river.

items.title: 'My kneecap was halfway up my thigh': How Sam came back from the brink

children.content: A career-threatening knee injury denied swimmer Sam Williamson the chance to defend his world title. Now he's back and looking to replicate the form that took him to the top of the world, by earning a place at the Commonwealth Games.

image.altText: Sam Williamson holds an Australian flag and looks up

items.title: After initial backlash, how do locals feel about the 'Blue Blob' now?

children.content: Cast in Blue has been the subject of criticism, ridicule and even vandalism over the past 10 months, but Mount Gambier's community has begun to embrace it.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black and white top and glasses stands next to a blue sculpture with her arm wrapped around its trunk.

items.title: 'Where there's a will there's a way': Thousands flock to National Folk Festival

children.content: The lure of a lively celebration of folk art outweighs concerns about the cost of fuel for thousands travelling to Canberra for the 60th National Folk Festival.

gallery.altText: A man dressed in an elaborate costume.

thumbnail.altText: A man dressed in an elaborate costume.
